title: chore(quest): bake quest workflow rules into issue body
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary

- GitHub strips `type: markdown` blocks from the created issue body — they only render on the form. That's why issues opened from this template don't show the assignment workflow.
- Move the rules into a required `process` textarea at the top of the form so they bake into every quest issue.
- Drop both unused `type: markdown` blocks.

## Test plan

- [x] YAML parses
- [x] Field order: process → goal → scope → acceptance → resources → parent-issue → reward
- [ ] Open a draft quest issue from the template and confirm the "How this quest works" block appears in the body

gist.headline: Quest rules baked in
gist.blurb: We've updated our GitHub quest templates so the workflow rules actually stick around in the issue body. No more lost instructions!
gist.summary: Moved the quest workflow rules from GitHub markdown blocks into a required textarea in the issue template so they persist in the created issue body.
gist.impact: Contributors will now see the assignment and workflow rules directly in the body of every newly opened quest issue.
gist.visual_concept: The bee (Polly) organizing or stamping a document/scroll (representing the issue template) with rules, while the Nomnom creature eats the old discarded markdown blocks.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot stamping a large parchment scroll with a lime green (#ecf874) wax seal. The round tan nomnom creature sits nearby happily eating discarded paper scraps. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
